points: A chemical equation uses specific formulas to symbolically represent active reactants and concluding products alongside their physical states (solid, liquid, gas, aqueous).
Equations must always be balanced to satisfy the law of conservation of mass, ensuring the total number of atoms for each element remains strictly equal on both sides.
Combination reactions locally unite multiple substances into a single product, while decomposition reactions break a single substance into simpler products using heat, light, or electricity.
Displacement reactions occur when a more reactive element replaces a less reactive one, whereas double displacement involves a mutual exchange of ions, often forming precipitates.
Redox reactions combine oxidation (gain of oxygen or loss of hydrogen) and reduction (loss of oxygen or gain of hydrogen) occurring simultaneously.
Exothermic reactions naturally release heat energy outward during product formation, whereas endothermic reactions actively require and absorb incoming environmental energy.
Corrosion gradually damages exposed metals through environmental oxidation (like the rusting of iron), and rancidity spoils unprotected fats and oils by severely altering their smell and taste.
